qa_pairs.question: What was the duration and purpose of the extended Cedar cluster outage that started on June 16, 2025, and how did it affect user access and services?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster experienced a planned extended outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025, to facilitate critical data-centre power and cooling upgrades for new system installations. During this period, login node access was unavailable, job submissions were suspended, and file systems were only accessible read-only via Globus. Specifically, during the week of June 23, read-only access was limited to the old project file system. Cedar Cloud remained operational, though brief interruptions occurred.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Rorqual compute cluster fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance, and which other clusters and cloud instances were not affected by this outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The Rorqual compute cluster was fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance on September 3, 2025, from 8:00 a.m. (EDT) until 5:00 p.m. (EDT). During this outage, the Narval cluster and cloud instances on the Béluga and Juno platforms were not affected.

qa_pairs.question: Which compute clusters and cloud instances were affected by the scheduled electrical maintenance outages on June 6-10, June 13-16, and April 30-May 1, 2025, and what services remained accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduled electrical maintenance outages on June 6-10, June 13-16, and April 30-May 1, 2025, required the shutdown of Béluga and Narval compute nodes, as well as cloud instances in the Juno Cloud's non-High Availability zone. During these times, Béluga cloud instances and Juno Cloud instances in the High Availability zone were not affected. Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible via Globus and login nodes, though intermittent disruptions occurred during the June 6-10 outage.
